title: SPECIALIZED VIRTUAL THERAPY FOR ADULTS IN FREDERICKSBURG, CHARLOTTESVILLE, AND ACROSS VIRGINIA

blocks.hero_title: Thoughtful Therapy That Meets You Where You Are.
blocks.hero_subtitle: SPECIALIZED VIRTUAL THERAPY FOR ADULTS IN FREDERICKSBURG, CHARLOTTESVILLE, AND ACROSS VIRGINIA

items.subtitle: Consistency Over Distance
items.text: Access specialized trauma care from Fredericksburg, Charlottesville, or anywhere in Virginia without the hours of drive time.

items.subtitle: Life-Proof Flexibility
items.text: Transition to a virtual session when a sick child, work emergency, or Virginia weather makes getting to the office impossible.

items.subtitle: Your Own Sanctuary
items.text: Engage in deep work like EMDR or IFS from the environment where you feel most secure.

blocks.subtitle: High-Touch Therapy, Designed for Your Life

blocks.body:
You know that sinking feeling when you realize you have to cancel therapy again because your kid is sick, work exploded, or the roads are a nightmare?

That pattern of canceling and rescheduling kills momentum. Your nervous system doesn't regulate in fits and starts. Trauma work requires consistency. And life is designed to destroy consistency.

That's where virtual therapy becomes your backup plan instead of your barrier.
blocks.title: When Life (or Distance) Gets in the Way
How We Use Virtual Sessions Strategically
blocks.body: I specialize in trauma-informed care that prioritizes deep connection. While being in the same room offers unique nuances, modern telehealth allows us to maintain a similar level of attunement from a distance.

blocks.subtitle: We utilize virtual sessions to ensure your work never stalls:

items.text: We may default to in-person sessions, but seamlessly pivot to virtual when life gets messy (e.g., sick kids, travel, or high-stress days where leaving home between work commitments is too much).
items.title: For Local Clients:
For Long-Distance (VA) Clients:
items.text: For those joining me from Fredericksburg, Charlottesville, Richmond, or other parts of Virginia, virtual sessions allow us to work together regardless of the miles. It means you can choose the therapist that feels like the right fit for you, without letting a 2-hour (plus) drive dictate your care.

blocks.body:
Therapy only works when you can consistently show up for it. But showing up gets exponentially harder when life is overwhelming you, or when the drive from Fredericksburg or Charlottesville starts to feel like a second job.

The clients who make the most sustainable progress aren’t the ones with perfect schedules. They're the ones who adapt when life gets messy instead of abandoning the work. Whether it’s a sick kid, a work emergency, or a 60-mile gap, virtual sessions ensure that your healing isn't at the mercy of your calendar or your commute.
blocks.title: Why This Flexibility Actually Matters

blocks.subtitle: Honesty is a core part of the work we do together.
blocks.body:
I won’t pretend that a screen is identical to being in the same room. Virtual sessions have a different rhythm. It is harder to co-regulate through a camera, we miss the subtle shifts in body language that happen when we’re face-to-face, and, quite frankly, technology glitches at the worst moments.

But here's why we use it: some of the most profound trauma work happens when you feel truly safe. For many, that sanctuary is their own home. While some intensive work genuinely benefits from being in-person, virtual sessions serve as a powerful tool to keep you connected to the work when the office feels out of reach. We don't let a screen (or a Wi-Fi signal) stand in the way of your momentum.
blocks.title: The Rhythm of Virtual Work

blocks.body:
We meet in person as your default. That's where the deepest work happens.

When life interferes, we switch to virtual for that session. You stay on track. We address what's happening. Then we return to in-person when circumstances allow.

This isn't about choosing virtual or in-person. It's about having both options so consistency doesn't become another thing life takes from you.

*For clients living further afield in Virginia, we design a custom hybrid or fully virtual plan that ensures you receive deep, consistent care without the burden of travel.*
blocks.image_alt: image of wood desk and computer screen in soft-lit office with table lamp on and large fig tree with basked filled with pillows
blocks.title: How Telehealth Works in Practice
Ready to Start?
blocks.body:
If you’re looking for therapy that adapts to your reality, whether that’s a busy schedule in Leesburg or a long-distance connection from southwestern Virginia, let’s talk.

**A quick note on geography: Due to state licensing regulations, all telehealth clients must be physically present in Virginia during telehealth appointments.**

blocks.title: Start Telehealth
blocks.image_alt: call to action button encouraging inquirer to schedule a consultation call
blocks.body:
### For our neighbors in Leesburg and Loudoun County:

We typically meet in person as our default. When life interferes—weather, illness, or travel—we seamlessly switch to virtual for that session. You stay on track, and we return to the office when circumstances allow.

### For our clients across Virginia:

For those joining from further afield, we design an intentional virtual or hybrid plan. This allows you to prioritize the "right fit" for your therapy without the burden of a three-hour drive.

**This isn't about choosing between convenience and quality. It’s about ensuring that consistency is a choice you get to make.**

subtitle: Thoughtful Therapy That Meets You Where You Are.
